visual_features.size: varies (from small to very big and tall)

intro_templates: It's a tree! Look how tall it is!
Wow, a tree! It has a big trunk. Can you see the leaves?
A big tree! It stands in the ground. Does it have many leaves?
Hello, tree! It has branches that reach out.
Look at this tree! Its leaves are green.

facts.text: Trees have a trunk and branches.
Trees grow from small seeds into big plants.
Many trees have green leaves in spring and summer.
Trees stand in the ground with their roots.
Tree bark feels rough when you touch it.

questions.text: What part of the tree do we call the trunk?
questions.hint: The thick part that goes up!

questions.text: What color are most tree leaves in summer?
Where do trees stand and grow?
How does tree bark feel to touch?
Do trees start as seeds or big plants?

intro_templates: It's a tree! Trees are like big homes for birds and squirrels!
Look at this tree! Do you know how we can tell how old a tree is?
Here's a tree! It is a living thing that helps clean the air we breathe.
Check out this tree! Its leaves use sunlight to make food for the tree.
Meet this tree! In autumn, its leaves can turn red, orange, and yellow!

facts.text: We can tell a tree's age by counting the rings inside its trunk.
Trees take in a gas we breathe out and give us the clean air we need.
Trees give animals like birds and squirrels a safe place to live.
Leaves use sunlight, air, and water to make food for the tree.
Some trees lose all their leaves in fall; these are called deciduous trees.

questions.text: How can we know how old a tree is?
What important thing do trees give us to breathe?
What do tree leaves use from the sun?
Name an animal that might live in a tree.
What happens to leaves on some trees in autumn?

intro_templates: It's a tree! Did you know that its roots do more than just hold it in place?
Look at this tree! It is part of a huge family of plants that has been on Earth for millions of years.
This is a tree. Losing leaves in fall is actually a smart way for it to save energy for winter.
Here we have a tree. The wood from its trunk can be used to build houses and make paper.
Consider this tree. What would happen if it didn't get any sunlight for a long time?

facts.text: Tree roots soak up water and food from the soil to help the tree grow.
Trees lose their leaves in autumn to save water and energy during the cold winter.
People use wood from trees to build houses, make furniture, and create paper.
If a tree gets no sunlight, its leaves cannot make food and the tree will become weak.
A very old tree can be a home for hundreds of insects, birds, and other small animals.

questions.text: What is one main job of a tree's roots?
Why do some trees lose leaves in fall?
What are two things we can make from tree wood?
questions.hint: Think of your home and books!

questions.text: What would happen to a tree without sunlight?
Why is an old tree important for animals?
questions.expected_answer: it provides a home for many

intro_templates: It's a flower! Look at its pretty petals!
Wow, a flower! It has a nice smell. Can you see the colors?
A beautiful flower! It grows on a stem with leaves.
Hello, flower! Let's count how many petals it has.
Look, a flower! Flowers make the world colorful.

facts.text: Flowers have colorful petals that we can see.
Flowers grow on stems with green leaves.
Many flowers have a sweet smell.
Bees and butterflies like to visit flowers.
We can plant flowers in gardens or pots.

questions.text: What color is this flower?
How many petals can you count on this flower?
What part of the flower holds it up?
Do bees like flowers?
Where can we grow flowers?
questions.hint: In soil, with sun and water!

intro_templates: It's a flower! Do you know that flowers make seeds to grow new plants?
Look at this flower! Its petals are arranged in a special pattern.
Here's a flower! Flowers need sun, water, and soil to grow healthy.
Check out this flower! Different flowers open at different times of day.
Meet this flower! Some flowers close their petals at night to sleep.

facts.text: Flowers make seeds inside them, which can grow into new plants.
The center of a flower often contains pollen, which looks like yellow powder.
Flowers need sunlight, water, and nutrients from soil to grow big and strong.
Some flowers open in the morning and close at night, like a daily routine.
Bees collect nectar from flowers to make honey, and they help flowers make seeds.

questions.text: What do flowers make that can grow into new plants?
What is the yellow powder in the center of some flowers called?
What three things do flowers need to grow?
questions.hint: Just like we need food and water!

questions.text: Why do some flowers close at night?
How do bees help flowers when they visit them?
questions.hint: They move pollen from flower to flower!

intro_templates: It's a flower! Did you know that flowers use their colors and scents to attract helpers?
Look at this flower! Each part of a flower has a special job in making seeds.
This is a flower. Flowers have male and female parts that work together.
Here we have a flower. Without flowers and their pollinators, we wouldn't have many fruits.
Consider this flower. Flowers have been on Earth for millions of years, changing and adapting.

facts.text: Flowers use bright colors and sweet scents to attract bees, butterflies, and other pollinators.
The male part of a flower makes pollen, and the female part makes ovules which become seeds after pollination.
When pollen moves from the male part to the female part of a flower, it is called pollination.
Many fruits we eat, like apples and strawberries, start as flowers before they grow into fruit.
Flowers can communicate with some insects by changing color or scent to show they are ready for pollination.

questions.text: Why are flowers often brightly colored and sweet-smelling?
questions.hint: They need helpers to make seeds!

questions.text: What is the process called when pollen moves to the female part of a flower?
What do many fruits start as before they become fruit?
questions.hint: First comes the flower, then the fruit!

questions.text: How do flowers 'talk' to insects?
What would happen if bees and butterflies didn't visit flowers?
questions.hint: Think about making seeds and fruits!

intro_templates: This is grass! It grows on the ground.
Look at the grass! It is green and soft.
Here is some grass. It covers the ground like a green carpet.
Touch the grass! It feels tickly on your feet.
Grass is everywhere! It is in parks and yards.

facts.text: Grass is green and grows on the ground.
Grass feels soft and tickly to touch.
Grass has long, thin blades.
You can walk and play on grass.
Grass needs water and sun to grow.

questions.text: What color is grass?
How does grass feel when you touch it?
Where does grass grow?
Can you walk on grass?
questions.hint: We play on it in the park!

questions.text: What does grass need to grow?

intro_templates: This is grass! Did you know it helps make the air clean?
Look at this grass! Cows and sheep love to eat it.
Here's some grass! It has roots that hold the soil together.
Check out this grass! Even after we walk on it, it can grow back.
Meet this grass! It can grow from tiny seeds into a green lawn.

facts.text: Grass helps clean the air by taking in carbon dioxide.
Many animals like cows, sheep, and rabbits eat grass.
Grass has roots under the soil that help prevent erosion.
When grass is cut, it can grow back again from the roots.
Grass produces seeds that can grow into new grass plants.

questions.text: How does grass help the air we breathe?
questions.hint: It takes in a gas we don't need.

questions.text: Name one animal that eats grass.
What part of grass is under the ground and holds it in place?
Can grass grow back after it is cut?
questions.hint: Lawns get mowed and grow again.

questions.text: How does a new grass plant start to grow?

intro_templates: This is grass! Did you know it's one of the most common plants on Earth?
Look at this grass! Its roots form a thick mat that helps stop soil from washing away.
Here's some grass! It turns sunlight into food through a process called photosynthesis.
Check out this grass! Even though each blade is thin, together they form a strong carpet.
Consider this grass. It can survive being stepped on because it grows from the base, not the tip.

facts.text: Grass is one of the most common plants on Earth, covering large areas called grasslands.
Grass roots form a dense network that holds soil firmly, preventing erosion during rain.
Grass makes its own food using sunlight, water, and air in a process called photosynthesis.
Grass grows from the bottom of the blade, which is why it can regrow after mowing or grazing.
Without grass, many animals would have nothing to eat, and soil would wash away more easily.

questions.text: Why is grass important for preventing soil erosion?
questions.hint: Think about what roots do in the ground.

questions.text: What three things does grass need to make its own food?
questions.hint: It uses sunlight like a power source.

questions.text: How can grass regrow after being cut or eaten?
questions.hint: It grows from a special part of the plant.

questions.text: What would happen to many animals if there was no grass?
questions.hint: Grass is a major food source.

questions.text: Is grass a common or rare plant on Earth?
questions.hint: It covers huge areas of land.

intro_templates: Look, a sunflower! It is big and yellow.
A sunflower! See its yellow petals and brown center.
Wow, this sunflower is so tall! Can you see it?
Hello, sunflower! It looks like the sun with yellow rays.
A bright sunflower! It has a long green stem.

facts.text: Sunflowers have big yellow petals.
The center of a sunflower is brown and round.
Sunflowers grow on long, green stems.
Sunflowers can be taller than a child.
Sunflowers turn their heads to face the sun.

questions.text: What color are sunflower petals?
questions.hint: They are bright like the sun!

questions.text: What is in the center of a sunflower?
How many petals does a sunflower have?
Are sunflowers tall or short plants?
What do sunflowers follow in the sky?

intro_templates: It's a sunflower! Did you know its center is made of many tiny flowers?
Look at this sunflower! It can produce seeds that birds and people like to eat.
Here's a sunflower! The flower head follows the sun across the sky each day.
Check out this sunflower! Its seeds are arranged in a special spiral pattern.
Meet this sunflower! It needs lots of sun and water to grow so big and tall.

facts.text: The brown center of a sunflower is made of many tiny flowers called disk florets.
Sunflower seeds grow in the brown center and are good food for birds and people.
Sunflower heads slowly turn during the day to face the sun as it moves.
Sunflower seeds are arranged in two spiral patterns that go in opposite directions.
Sunflowers need lots of sunlight, water, and space to grow healthy and strong.

questions.text: What is the brown center of a sunflower made of?
What can grow in the center of a sunflower?
Why does a sunflower turn its head during the day?
How are sunflower seeds arranged in the center?
questions.hint: They curve around in two ways!

questions.text: What three things do sunflowers need to grow well?
questions.hint: They love sun, need water, and room to grow!

intro_templates: It's a sunflower! Do you know that the spiral pattern of its seeds follows a special mathematical rule?
Look at this sunflower! Even when it's cloudy, the flower head still knows where the sun should be.
This is a sunflower. After it finishes following the sun, it faces east and stays that way.
Here we have a sunflower. The oil from its seeds can be used for cooking and making other things.
Consider this sunflower. It can help clean the soil by taking in bad things through its roots.

facts.text: The spiral pattern of sunflower seeds follows the Fibonacci sequence, a special number pattern found in nature.
Young sunflowers track the sun from east to west each day, but older flowers stop and face east.
Sunflower oil is made from the seeds and is used for cooking, making margarine, and even in some paints.
Sunflowers can help clean polluted soil by absorbing harmful metals and chemicals through their roots.
A single sunflower can produce over 1,000 seeds, providing food for many animals and people.

questions.text: What special number pattern do sunflower seed spirals follow?
questions.hint: It's a pattern of adding the last two numbers!

questions.text: Do old sunflowers still follow the sun all day?
questions.hint: They stop moving and face one direction.

questions.text: What are three uses for sunflower oil?
questions.hint: People cook with it, make spreads, and artists use it!

questions.text: How can sunflowers help a dirty soil area?
questions.expected_answer: clean it by absorbing bad stuff
questions.hint: They take in harmful things through roots!

questions.text: If a sunflower makes 1,000 seeds, how many animals could it feed?
questions.hint: Lots of birds, squirrels, and people could share!

intro_templates: It's a rose! Look at its pretty petals!
Wow, a rose! It smells so nice. What color is it?
A beautiful rose! It has soft petals and a green stem.
Hello, rose! It has sharp thorns on its stem. Be careful!
Look at this flower! It is a rose. It grows in gardens.

facts.text: Roses have soft, colorful petals.
Roses grow on stems with green leaves.
Rose stems have sharp thorns to protect them.
Roses can be red, pink, white, or yellow.
Roses smell very sweet and nice.

questions.text: What color is this rose?
How do rose petals feel?
What grows on a rose stem that can poke you?
How does a rose smell?
Where do roses often grow?

intro_templates: It's a rose! Roses are popular flowers that people give as gifts.
Look at this rose! Did you know bees love to visit roses for nectar?
Here's a rose! Roses need sunlight and water to grow well.
Check out this rose! The thorns help keep hungry animals from eating the plant.
Meet this rose! Roses can bloom again and again during the warm season.

facts.text: Bees and butterflies visit roses to drink sweet nectar from the flowers.
People often give roses as gifts to show love or say thank you.
Roses need plenty of sunlight and regular water to stay healthy and bloom.
The sharp thorns on rose stems help protect the plant from animals that might eat it.
Roses can bloom many times from spring until fall if they are well cared for.

questions.text: Why do bees and butterflies like to visit roses?
questions.hint: They are looking for sweet food!

questions.text: Why do people give roses as gifts?
questions.hint: It's a way to share feelings!

questions.text: What two things do roses need most to grow well?
How do thorns help the rose plant?
When do roses bloom?

intro_templates: It's a rose! Did you know roses are related to apples and strawberries?
Look at this rose! Roses can be used to make perfumes and rose water.
This is a rose. The petals can change color slightly as the flower ages.
Here we have a rose. Some roses are bred to have no thorns for safer handling.
Consider this rose. The rose is the national flower of several countries, including the USA and England.

facts.text: Roses are part of the same plant family as apples, strawberries, and almonds.
Rose petals can be used to make perfume, rose water, and even some teas.
As a rose flower gets older, its petals might change color or wilt and fall off.
Some special rose varieties have been grown to have very few or no thorns at all.
The rose is the national flower of the United States, England, and several other countries.

questions.text: What fruits are in the same plant family as roses?
What can people make from rose petals?
What happens to rose petals as the flower gets old?
Can all roses have thorns?
questions.expected_answer: no, some have few or none

questions.text: Why might a country choose the rose as its national flower?
questions.hint: Because it is beautiful and loved!

intro_templates: It's bamboo! Look at its tall, straight stems!
Wow, bamboo! It has joints like our fingers have joints.
A green bamboo plant! Can you see its long thin leaves?
Hello, bamboo! It grows in groups. Count how many stems you see!
Look, bamboo! It feels smooth and hard when you touch it.

facts.text: Bamboo has tall, green stems with joints.
Bamboo leaves are long, thin, and green.
Bamboo grows in groups with many stems together.
Bamboo stems are hollow inside, like a straw.
Pandas love to eat bamboo leaves and stems.

questions.text: What color is bamboo?
questions.hint: Look at the stems and leaves!

questions.text: How do bamboo stems feel?
What animal loves to eat bamboo?
Are bamboo stems hollow or solid inside?
Do bamboo plants grow alone or in groups?

intro_templates: It's bamboo! Do you know bamboo is actually a type of grass, not a tree?
Look at this bamboo! It's one of the fastest growing plants in the world.
Here's bamboo! People use bamboo to make many useful things.
Check out this bamboo! New bamboo grows from shoots that come out of the ground.
Meet bamboo! Even though it looks like wood, it's different in many ways.

facts.text: Bamboo is the fastest growing plant in the world.
Bamboo is a type of grass, not a tree, even though it looks like one.
People use bamboo to make furniture, floors, and even musical instruments.
New bamboo plants grow from shoots that push up through the soil.
Bamboo can grow in many different places, from hot jungles to cold mountains.

questions.text: Is bamboo a type of grass or a type of tree?
questions.hint: Even though it's tall like a tree!

questions.text: How fast does bamboo grow compared to other plants?
What are three things people make from bamboo?
questions.hint: Think about things in your house!

questions.text: How does new bamboo start to grow?
questions.hint: They come up from the ground!

questions.text: Can bamboo grow in cold places?
questions.hint: Some types can grow on mountains!

intro_templates: It's bamboo! Did you know bamboo helps clean the air we breathe?
Look at this bamboo! Its hollow stems make it light but very strong.
This is bamboo. Some types of bamboo only flower once every 100 years!
Here we have bamboo. It's a sustainable plant because it grows back quickly after being cut.
Consider this bamboo. Its root system helps prevent soil from washing away.

facts.text: Bamboo helps clean the air by taking in carbon dioxide and giving out oxygen.
Bamboo is very strong for its weight because of its hollow stem structure.
Some bamboo plants flower only once every 50 to 100 years, then they die.
Bamboo roots spread underground and help hold soil together to prevent erosion.
Bamboo can be harvested without killing the plant because it grows back from the roots.

questions.text: How does bamboo help clean the air?
questions.hint: Think about what plants breathe in and out!

questions.text: Why is bamboo strong but lightweight?
questions.hint: What's special about its stem structure?

questions.text: How often do some bamboo plants flower?
How do bamboo roots help the soil?
If we cut bamboo, will it grow back?

intro_templates: It's a mushroom! See its round cap.
Look, a mushroom! It grows on the ground.
A little mushroom! It has a stem and a cap.
Hello, mushroom! It looks like a small umbrella.
Wow, a mushroom! Can you see its color?

facts.text: Mushrooms grow on the ground or on trees.
A mushroom has a stem and a cap.
Mushrooms come in many colors like brown, white, and red.
Mushrooms feel soft and spongy when you touch them.
Some mushrooms are very small, and some are bigger.

questions.text: Where do mushrooms grow?
How many main parts does a mushroom have?
What color is this mushroom?
How does a mushroom feel when you touch it?
Is this mushroom big or small?

intro_templates: It's a mushroom! Do you know that mushrooms are not plants? They are fungi!
Look at this mushroom! It can grow very fast after it rains.
Here's a mushroom! Did you know some mushrooms can be eaten but some are poisonous?
Check out this mushroom! The part under the cap has thin lines called gills.
Meet this mushroom! Mushrooms help break down old leaves and wood in the forest.

facts.text: Mushrooms are not plants; they belong to a group called fungi.
Mushrooms grow from tiny spores, not from seeds like plants.
Some mushrooms are safe to eat, but some are poisonous and dangerous.
Under the cap, many mushrooms have thin lines called gills.
Mushrooms help recycle nutrients by breaking down dead things in nature.

questions.text: Are mushrooms plants?
How do mushrooms grow new mushrooms?
Can we eat all mushrooms we find?
What are the thin lines under the mushroom cap called?
What do mushrooms do in the forest?

intro_templates: It's a mushroom! Did you know that mushrooms are more closely related to animals than to plants?
Look at this mushroom! The part we see is just the fruit; the main body is hidden underground.
This is a mushroom. They can't make their own food like plants, so they have to get it from other sources.
Here we have a mushroom. Some mushrooms grow in a circle called a 'fairy ring', which looks magical!
Consider this mushroom. Even though they don't have seeds, they spread spores that are like dust in the air.

facts.text: The mushroom we see is just the fruiting body; the main part is a network of threads hidden in the soil.
Mushrooms cannot make their own food from sunlight like plants do; they get food from decaying matter.
Sometimes mushrooms grow in a circle called a 'fairy ring', which was once thought to be made by fairies dancing.
Mushrooms are more closely related to animals than to plants in the tree of life.
Spores from mushrooms are so tiny and light that they can float in the air like dust.

questions.text: Is the mushroom we see the whole plant?
Why can't mushrooms make food from sunlight like green plants?
What is a circle of mushrooms called?
Do mushrooms belong to animals or plants?
questions.expected_answer: No, Mushrooms belong to the category of large fungi.

questions.text: How do mushroom spores travel to new places?

intro_templates: It's a cactus! It has sharp spines. Be careful!
Look, a cactus! It is green and lives in dry places.
A prickly plant! Can you see its spines?
Hello, cactus! It stores water inside its thick body.
This plant is special. It doesn't need much water.

facts.text: A cactus has sharp spines for protection.
Cacti are usually green and have thick stems.
They live in hot, dry places called deserts.
Some cacti grow pretty flowers.
Cacti do not have flat leaves like most plants.

questions.text: What does a cactus have that is sharp?
What color are most cacti?
Do cacti need a lot of water?
Where do cacti usually live?
Can you touch a cactus with your hand?

intro_templates: It's a cactus! Its spines are actually modified leaves. They help it survive in the desert.
Look at this cactus! It stores water inside its thick, fleshy stem for dry times.
Here's a cactus! Did you know it grows very slowly and can live for many years?
Check out this cactus! Even though it's dry where it lives, it can bloom beautiful flowers.
Meet this cactus! Its roots are wide but shallow to catch any rain quickly.

facts.text: Cactus spines protect it from animals and help shade it from the hot sun.
The thick, green stem stores water like a big water bottle.
Cacti have wide, shallow roots to soak up rainwater fast before it dries.
They grow very slowly, sometimes only one centimeter a year.
Birds and insects help pollinate cactus flowers so they can make seeds.

questions.text: Why does a cactus have sharp spines?
questions.hint: To keep animals away and stay cool.

questions.text: Where does a cactus store water?
Do cacti have deep roots like a tree?
Do cacti grow fast or slow?
Who helps carry pollen between cactus flowers?

intro_templates: It's a cactus! Its spines do more than just protect it; they also help collect water from fog.
Look at this cactus! It has a special way of breathing at night to save water.
This is a cactus. Its waxy skin is like a raincoat, keeping the water inside from evaporating.
Here we have a cactus. Because it stores so much water, it can be very heavy!
Consider this cactus. If it gets too much water, it can actually get sick and rot.

facts.text: Cactus spines can collect tiny water droplets from fog and morning dew, guiding them to the roots.
Cacti open their pores only at night to take in carbon dioxide, which helps them lose less water.
A waxy coating on the stem acts like a seal, keeping the stored water from drying out.
A large saguaro cactus can weigh as much as a car because it holds so much water.
If a cactus in a pot gets too much water, its roots can rot because it is not used to it.

questions.text: How can spines help a cactus get water, besides protection?
questions.hint: They can catch tiny drops from the air.

questions.text: Why do cacti do most of their breathing at night?
questions.hint: The hot sun would steal their water.

questions.text: What is the waxy skin on a cactus for?
questions.hint: It stops the water from escaping.

questions.text: Why can a big cactus be very heavy?
questions.hint: Water is heavy, and it's like a tank.

questions.text: What might happen if you water a potted cactus too often?
questions.hint: It's like giving it too much of a good thing.

intro_templates: Look, a lotus flower! It grows in the water.
It's a beautiful lotus! See its big, round leaves?
A pink lotus! Can you count its petals?
Hello, lotus! It smells very nice.
Wow, a lotus! It stands tall above the water.

facts.text: Lotus flowers are often pink or white.
Lotus plants grow in water, like in ponds.
Lotus leaves are big, flat, and round like a plate.
The flower grows on a long, straight stem.
Lotus flowers have many, many soft petals.

questions.text: Where does a lotus grow?
questions.hint: It likes to be in ponds or lakes!

questions.text: What color is this lotus flower?
questions.hint: Is it pink, white, or another color?

questions.text: What shape are lotus leaves?
questions.hint: They look like a big plate!

questions.text: Does a lotus have many petals or just a few?
questions.hint: Look, there are lots of them!

questions.text: Can you point to the stem of the lotus?
questions.hint: It's the long part holding the flower up!

intro_templates: It's a lotus! Its leaves have a magic trick – water rolls right off!
Look at this lotus! After the petals fall, a funny seed pod is left.
Here's a lotus! People love it because it grows from mud but is very clean.
Check out this lotus! It closes its petals at night and opens in the morning.
Meet the lotus! Its roots are buried in the mud at the bottom of the pond.

facts.text: Water forms beads and rolls off lotus leaves, never making them wet.
After the flower is gone, a brown seed pod is left. It looks like a showerhead with holes.
Lotus plants grow from roots in muddy water, but the flowers and leaves stay clean.
The lotus flower closes its petals at night and opens again with the sun.
People in many countries think the lotus is a very special and pure flower.

questions.text: What happens when water falls on a lotus leaf?
questions.hint: It doesn't get the leaf wet!

questions.text: What is left after the lotus petals fall off?
Where are the roots of a lotus plant?
questions.hint: At the bottom of the pond!

questions.text: When does the lotus flower close its petals?
Is the lotus flower clean even though it grows in mud?
questions.hint: It stays very clean and beautiful!

intro_templates: It's a lotus! Scientists love how its leaves stay so clean – it's called the 'lotus effect'.
Look at this lotus! Its seeds can sleep in the mud for a very, very long time and still grow.
This is a lotus. In stories, it often stands for beauty and strength that comes from a difficult place.
Here we have a lotus. What do you think would happen if its leaves didn't repel water?
Consider this lotus. How do you think its seeds travel to make new plants in other ponds?

facts.text: The lotus leaf has a special bumpy surface that makes water droplets pick up dirt and roll away.
Lotus seeds are famous for living a long time. Some have grown after sleeping in mud for 1000 years!
Because it grows from muddy water but stays clean, people see the lotus as a symbol of purity and overcoming challenges.
If lotus leaves got wet and dirty, they might not get enough sunlight or could get sick from germs.
The holes in the old seed pod let the hard seeds fall out, float on water, or be carried by birds to new places.

questions.text: Why does water roll off a lotus leaf so easily?
questions.hint: Think about the leaf's special skin!

questions.text: How long can some lotus seeds wait in the mud before growing?
Why do you think people use the lotus to mean 'staying pure in a dirty place'?
questions.hint: It grows in mud but looks clean.

questions.text: What might happen if a lotus leaf could not repel water?
questions.hint: Would it stay as clean and healthy?

questions.text: How might a lotus seed travel from one pond to another?

intro_templates: It's a dandelion! Look at its yellow flower!
Wow, a dandelion! It has a bright yellow head.
A little dandelion! See its tall thin stem?
Hello, dandelion! It has leaves with sharp teeth.
Look! A dandelion puffball. Blow on it and make a wish!

facts.text: Dandelions have bright yellow flower heads.
Dandelion leaves have jagged edges like lion's teeth.
Dandelion stems are long, thin, and hollow inside.
Each dandelion puffball has many tiny seeds.
You can blow on a dandelion puffball to scatter the seeds.

questions.text: What color is a dandelion flower?
How do dandelion leaves look at the edges?
Is a dandelion stem hollow or solid inside?
What do we call the white fluffy part of a dandelion?
What can you do with a dandelion puffball?

intro_templates: It's a dandelion! Do you know that every part of this plant can be used?
Look at this dandelion! Its white puffball is actually made up of many tiny seeds.
Here's a dandelion! Did you know dandelions can grow almost anywhere?
Check out this dandelion! Its yellow flower turns into a white puffball when it gets old.
Meet this dandelion! Bees love to visit dandelions to collect pollen.

facts.text: A dandelion's yellow flower changes into a white puffball full of seeds when it matures.
Each white puffball contains hundreds of tiny seeds, each with a feathery parachute.
Dandelions have a long taproot that goes deep into the ground to find water.
Bees and other insects visit dandelion flowers to collect nectar and pollen.
You can eat young dandelion leaves in salads, and they taste a little bitter.

questions.text: What happens to a dandelion's yellow flower when it gets old?
How do dandelion seeds travel through the air?
questions.hint: Each seed has a fluffy part!

questions.text: Why do dandelions have long roots?
questions.hint: They grow deep into the soil!

questions.text: Which insects like to visit dandelion flowers?
Can people eat any part of the dandelion plant?

intro_templates: It's a dandelion! Did you know that dandelions are one of the first flowers to bloom in spring, providing important food for bees?
Look at this dandelion! Its seeds can travel long distances by wind, which is why dandelions grow everywhere.
This is a dandelion. Its deep taproot helps break up hard soil and brings nutrients to the surface.
Here we have a dandelion. Every part of this plant has a use, from leaves to roots, and even the flowers.
Consider this dandelion. It can tell time! The flowers open in the morning and close in the evening.

facts.text: Dandelion flowers open wide in the morning sunlight and close in the evening or on cloudy days.
The fluffy parachute on each dandelion seed helps it catch the wind and travel far away from the parent plant.
Dandelions are important early spring flowers that provide nectar and pollen for bees when few other flowers are blooming.
Dandelion roots can be dried and roasted to make a coffee-like drink that does not have caffeine.
A dandelion plant can produce over 2,000 seeds in a single year, which is why they spread so quickly.

questions.text: When do dandelion flowers usually open and close?
questions.expected_answer: open in morning, close in evening

questions.text: Why do dandelion seeds have fluffy parachutes?
Why are dandelions important for bees in early spring?
What can be made from dried and roasted dandelion roots?
questions.hint: It's a hot drink but not coffee!

questions.text: About how many seeds can one dandelion plant produce in a year?

intro_templates: It's a pine tree! Look at its green needles!
Wow, a pine tree! It stays green all year. Can you see the cones?
A tall pine tree! It has pointy leaves called needles.
Hello, pine tree! It feels rough when you touch its bark.
Look at this tree! Pine trees smell fresh and nice.

facts.text: Pine trees have green needle leaves.
Pine trees have brown, rough bark.
Pine trees make pine cones.
Pine trees stay green all year long.
Pine trees can grow very tall.

questions.text: What color are pine tree leaves?
How does pine tree bark feel?
What part of the pine tree is brown and hard?
Do pine trees lose all leaves in winter?
Are pine trees usually short or tall?

intro_templates: It's a pine tree! Do you know that pine trees make seeds inside their cones?
Look at this pine tree! Its needles are special leaves that help it save water.
Here's a pine tree! Did you know that pine trees can live for a very long time?
Check out this pine tree! Some animals, like squirrels, eat the seeds from pine cones.
Meet this pine tree! People sometimes use pine trees as Christmas trees because they stay green.

facts.text: Pine trees keep their green needles all through winter.
Pine cones protect the seeds of the pine tree inside them.
Pine trees can live for many years, even hundreds of years.
Squirrels and birds eat the seeds from pine cones.
Pine trees have a sticky sap that can help heal cuts on the tree.

questions.text: What do pine cones protect inside them?
questions.hint: Baby pine trees grow from these!

questions.text: Can pine trees stay green in winter?
What animal likes to eat pine cone seeds?
questions.hint: A small animal with a bushy tail!

questions.text: What sticky stuff do pine trees make to heal cuts?
How long can pine trees live?

intro_templates: It's a pine tree! Did you know that pine trees are part of a group called conifers?
Look at this pine tree! Its needle shape helps it survive in cold and dry places.
This is a pine tree. The pine cones open up when it's warm and dry to release seeds.
Here we have a pine tree. Pine trees can grow in sandy soil where other trees cannot.
Consider this pine tree. People use wood from pine trees to build houses and make paper.

facts.text: Pine trees are conifers, which means they have cones instead of flowers.
The needle shape of pine leaves helps the tree save water in cold or dry weather.
Pine cones open up on warm, dry days to let the seeds out to grow new trees.
Pine trees can grow in poor soil because they don't need as many nutrients as other trees.
Pine wood is used to make many things like furniture, paper, and the frames of houses.

questions.text: What group of trees do pine trees belong to?
Why are pine tree leaves shaped like needles?
When do pine cones open to release seeds?
Can pine trees grow in sandy soil?
What are two things made from pine wood?
questions.hint: Think about things in your house!
